29:     case "is not":
30:     case "is none of":
31:       return !found;
32:   }

35: export function multiOptionFilterFn(
36:   inputData: string[],
37:   filterValue: FilterModel<"multiOption">,
38: ) {
39:   if (!inputData) return false;
40: 
41:   if (
42:     filterValue.values.length === 0 ||
43:     !filterValue.values[0] ||
44:     filterValue.values[0].length === 0
45:   )
46:     return true;
47: 
48:   const values = inputData;
49:   const filterValues = filterValue.values;
50: 
51:   switch (filterValue.operator) {
52:     case "include":
53:     case "include any of":
54:       return intersection(values, filterValues).length > 0;
55:     case "exclude":
56:       return intersection(values, filterValues).length === 0;
57:     case "exclude if any of":
58:       return !(intersection(values, filterValues).length > 0);
59:     case "include all of":
60:       return intersection(values, filterValues).length === filterValues.length;
61:     case "exclude if all":
62:       return !(
63:         intersection(values, filterValues).length === filterValues.length
64:       );
65:   }
66: }

68: export function dateFilterFn<TData>(
69:   inputData: Date,
70:   filterValue: FilterModel<"date">,
71: ) {
72:   if (!filterValue || filterValue.values.length === 0) return true;
73: 
74:   if (
75:     dateFilterOperators[filterValue.operator].target === "single" &&
76:     filterValue.values.length > 1
77:   )
78:     throw new Error("Singular operators require at most one filter value");
79: 
80:   if (
81:     filterValue.operator in ["is between", "is not between"] &&
82:     filterValue.values.length !== 2
83:   )
84:     throw new Error("Plural operators require two filter values");
85: 
86:   const filterVals = filterValue.values;
87:   const d1 = filterVals[0];
88:   const d2 = filterVals[1];
89: 
90:   const value = inputData;
91: 
92:   switch (filterValue.operator) {
93:     case "is":
94:       return isSameDay(value, d1);
95:     case "is not":
96:       return !isSameDay(value, d1);
97:     case "is before":
98:       return isBefore(value, startOfDay(d1));
99:     case "is on or after":
100:       return isSameDay(value, d1) || isAfter(value, startOfDay(d1));
101:     case "is after":
102:       return isAfter(value, startOfDay(d1));
103:     case "is on or before":
104:       return isSameDay(value, d1) || isBefore(value, startOfDay(d1));
105:     case "is between":
106:       return isWithinInterval(value, {
107:         end: endOfDay(d2),
108:         start: startOfDay(d1),
109:       });
110:     case "is not between":
111:       return !isWithinInterval(value, {
112:         end: endOfDay(filterValue.values[1]),
113:         start: startOfDay(filterValue.values[0]),
114:       });
115:   }

125:   const filterStr = filterValue.values[0].toLowerCase().trim();
126: 
127:   if (filterStr === "") return true;
128: 
129:   const found = value.includes(filterStr);
130: 
131:   switch (filterValue.operator) {
132:     case "contains":
133:       return found;
134:     case "does not contain":
135:       return !found;
136:   }

139: export function numberFilterFn<TData>(

141:   filterValue: FilterModel<"number">,
142: ) {

144:     return true;
145:   }
146: 
147:   const value = inputData;
148:   const filterVal = filterValue.values[0];
149: 
150:   switch (filterValue.operator) {
151:     case "is":
152:       return value === filterVal;
153:     case "is not":
154:       return value !== filterVal;
155:     case "is greater than":
156:       return value > filterVal;
157:     case "is greater than or equal to":
158:       return value >= filterVal;
159:     case "is less than":
160:       return value < filterVal;
161:     case "is less than or equal to":
162:       return value <= filterVal;
163:     case "is between": {
164:       const lowerBound = filterValue.values[0];
165:       const upperBound = filterValue.values[1];
166:       return value >= lowerBound && value <= upperBound;
167:     }
168:     case "is not between": {
169:       const lowerBound = filterValue.values[0];
170:       const upperBound = filterValue.values[1];
171:       return value < lowerBound || value > upperBound;
172:     }
173:     default:
174:       return true;

19:   isPending: () => boolean;
20: };

22: export type DebouncedState<T extends (...args: any) => ReturnType<T>> = ((
23:   ...args: Parameters<T>
24: ) => ReturnType<T> | undefined) &
25:   ControlFunctions;
26: 
27: export function useDebounceCallback<T extends (...args: any) => ReturnType<T>>(
28:   func: T,
29:   delay = 500,
30:   options?: DebounceOptions,
31: ): DebouncedState<T> {
32:   const debouncedFunc = useRef<ReturnType<typeof debounce>>(null);
33: 
34:   useUnmount(() => {
35:     if (debouncedFunc.current) {
36:       debouncedFunc.current.cancel();
37:     }
38:   });
39: 
40:   const debounced = useMemo(() => {
41:     const debouncedFuncInstance = debounce(func, delay, options);
42: 
43:     const wrappedFunc: DebouncedState<T> = (...args: Parameters<T>) => {
44:       return debouncedFuncInstance(...args);
45:     };
46: 
47:     wrappedFunc.cancel = () => {
48:       debouncedFuncInstance.cancel();
49:     };
50: 
51:     wrappedFunc.isPending = () => {
52:       return !!debouncedFunc.current;
53:     };
54: 
55:     wrappedFunc.flush = () => {
56:       return debouncedFuncInstance.flush();
57:     };
58: 
59:     return wrappedFunc;
60:   }, [func, delay, options]);
61: 
62:   // Update the debounced function ref whenever func, wait, or options change
63:   useEffect(() => {
64:     debouncedFunc.current = debounce(func, delay, options);
65:   }, [func, delay, options]);
66: 
67:   return debounced;

11: function deepEqual(a: unknown, b: unknown): boolean {
12:   if (a === b) return true;
13:   if (a === null || b === null) return false;

17:   if (aKeys.length !== bKeys.length) return false;
18:   for (const key of aKeys) {
19:     if (!bKeys.includes(key)) return false;
20:     if (
21:       !deepEqual(
22:         (a as Record<string, unknown>)[key],
23:         (b as Record<string, unknown>)[key],
24:       )
25:     )
26:       return false;
27:   }
28:   return true;
29: }
